Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm, this is a detailed request about interpreting a DECLARE model for a loan approval process. The user wants a step-by-step explanation of how the constraints translate into real-world workflow, including motivations and consequences of deviations.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The DECLARE model is quite comprehensive with 18 constraint types, so I need to systematically map each constraint to practical process behaviors. I should start by outlining the core workflow sequence, then group constraints by their functional purpose like sequencing rules, exclusions, and interdependencies.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For clarity, I can structure the response into three parts: first, walk through the ideal process flow; second, explain how constraints enforce order and compliance; third, discuss real-world implications of violations. The forbidden activity Proceed Without Compliance deserves special emphasis since it is a hard stop.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The challenge is making the technical constraints intuitive maybe use analogies like gatekeepers precedence or synchronization coexistence. I will avoid jargon overload and tie each constraint to business logic, like risk management or regulatory needs.
